title: Iran Conflict Day 65: Tehran University Bombing Site to Become War Museum as Civilian Impact Mounts
summary: Regional observer reports on the civilian dimension of the Iran war, including plans to convert the bombed Isfahan University of Technology into a war museum documenting the conflict's impact on education.
contents:
## Iran War Civilian Impact — Day 65 Observations

### University Museum Announcement
Isfahan University of Technology announced plans to preserve its bombing site as a war museum documenting the conflict's impact on Iranian education and science. University chief Zafarollah Kalantari stated that "the current damaged site will stand preserved as a war museum to remain a document of the country's scientific oppression in history." The university suffered approximately $11 million in damage from US-Israeli strikes in March.

### Educational Infrastructure Damage
More than 30 Iranian universities, including institutions in Tehran, have sustained targeting during the conflict. The decision to convert damaged sites into memorials reflects the regime's strategy of documenting civilian infrastructure losses for both domestic and international audiences.

### Bushehr Nuclear Facility Area
Reports indicate another strike hit an area near the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, with one security guard killed according to Iran's Atomic Energy Organization. The proximity of strikes to nuclear facilities continues raising international concerns about escalation risks and environmental safety.

### Civilian Economic Impact
The conflict's economic effects extend across the region, with Iran's currency under severe pressure and trade disruptions affecting neighboring economies. France reported building materials price increases affecting over 60 percent of small construction companies due to supply chain disruptions linked to the Middle East conflict. OPEC+ member countries plan discussions on oil production for June as the conflict reshapes global energy markets.
